sections.paragraph:
φάντασμα, ατος, τό (Aeschyl., Pla. et al.; LXX; En 99:7; TestSol 8:9 C; Philo; Jos., Bell. 5, 381, Ant. 5, 21 3; Tat. 7:3) apparition, esp. ghost (Aeschyl. et al.; Pla., Phd. 81d, Tim. 71a; Dionys. Hal. 4, 62; Plut., Dio 2, 4; Lucian, Philops. 29; PGM 4, 2701; 7, 579 φυλακτήριον πρὸς δαίμονας, πρὸς πᾶσαν νόσον καὶ πάθος; Job 20:8 v.l.; Wsd 17:14; Jos., Ant. 1, 331; 333; Tat. 7, 3 s. Reader, Polemo 376) Mt 14:26; Mk 6:49 (on these two pass. cp. Phlegon: 257 Fgm. 36 II, 3 Jac.); Lk 24:37 D.—FAltheim, ARW 27, 1929, 48.—DELG s.v. φαίνω B 10. M-M. TW. Sv.

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

En En = I Enoch—List 2

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

esp. esp. = especially

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Reader, Polemo Reader, Polemo , s. Polemo—List 5

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Phlegon Phlegon , II a.d.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

ARW ARW = Archiv für Religionswissenschaft—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1049
